lesson.menu_desc: Reading a customer success plan + sequencing words -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 6 -- Onboarding & Driving Adoption
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Patrícia Xavier Lima Iwashita | Aula 6 -- Onboarding & Driving Adoption

reading.rtitle: Your Customer Success Plan — A Step-by-Step Guide
paras: Welcome to your success plan. This short guide explains how we help a new customer get real value from the platform, step by step. Our goal is simple: to enable your team to work with confidence and to reach the results you care about. Every customer is different, so first we agree on your main goals, and then we build the plan around them.

The plan follows a clear order. First, we run a kickoff call and confirm who your internal champion will be. Then, our team helps you configure the platform and set up your first license. After that, we book a hands-on walkthrough, so your users can see exactly how the product works. Once your users feel comfortable, we move on to training and share the best practices that other teams use every day.

Adoption does not happen by accident; it grows when people use the product and see the value for themselves. For that reason, we review your success plan every month and check your progress against your goals. If something is not working, we adjust the next steps together. Finally, when your team is fully onboarded, we celebrate the wins and plan the next phase of growth.
reading.source: Adapted for class use from a customer onboarding success plan

gist.prompt: What is the best one-line summary of this guide?
choices: It is a price list for software licenses and training sessions.
It describes a step-by-step success plan that helps a new customer adopt the product and reach their goals.
It explains how to fix bugs after a customer complains about the platform.

items: The first step in the plan is a kickoff call and choosing an internal champion.
First, the team runs a kickoff call and confirms the customer's champion.
Training happens before the users feel comfortable with the product.
Training comes once the users feel comfortable, after the walkthrough.
The team reviews the success plan every month to check progress.
They review the plan every month and check progress against the goals.
Adoption happens automatically, even when people do not use the product.
Adoption grows only when people use the product and see the value.
In your own onboardings, which step is the hardest for customers, and why?
The guide says adoption grows when users see value. How do you show value to a new customer?
If a customer skipped the walkthrough, what problems might appear later in adoption?

listenings.text: Hi Patricia, this is Mike from the onboarding team, leaving you a quick voice note before our kickoff call so we are on the same page. Here is the order I suggest for the new account. First, on the call, let us confirm their main goals and who their internal champion will be, because adoption is so much easier when one person owns it on their side. Then, we help them configure the platform and set up the first license, so the tool is ready to use. After that, I will run a hands-on walkthrough with their users, so they see exactly how the product works in real life. Once they feel comfortable, we move on to proper training and share the best practices our strongest teams use every day. The whole idea is to drive real adoption, not just to switch the software on. If they actually use it and see the value, the renewal takes care of itself. Talk soon, and send me their goals before the call if you can.

Hi Patricia, this is Sofia, the admin on the customer side, leaving you a short message after our first month on the plan. I just wanted to say thank you, because the step-by-step approach really worked for us. The kickoff call was clear, and naming a champion was a great idea; the whole team now asks me first instead of guessing. The walkthrough was the moment things clicked for my users. Before it, people were nervous about the new tool, but once they saw how simple it was to configure their own dashboards, they relaxed. The training and the best practices you shared have saved us a lot of time. If I am honest, we still have one weak spot. Two people in finance have not really started using the product yet, so their adoption is low. Could we add a small extra walkthrough just for them in next month's plan? Apart from that, we are very happy. We can already see the value, and we would love to talk about the next phase. Thanks again for all the support.
